---
title: "Alcohol Arguments: Challenging Return-to-Use Thoughts"
format: html
---

# Alcohol Arguments

"Alcohol arguments" are persuasive thoughts that try to justify drinking. They often sound logical in the moment: "I've been good, I deserve it," "Just tonight won't matter," or "I need this to relax." These thoughts are part of addictive thinking, which tends to focus on short-term relief and minimize long-term cost.

Cognitive-behavioral recovery tools teach you to answer these arguments with evidence, not emotion alone. When you prepare responses in advance, you are less likely to get pulled into bargaining during high-risk moments.

Relapse justification tends to use selective attention: it magnifies short-term relief while minimizing consequences. Writing out balanced responses strengthens cognitive control and helps interrupt permission-giving thoughts before they become cravings.

## Thought Rebuttal Exercise

| Alcohol argument | What it leaves out | My balanced recovery response |
|------------------|--------------------|-------------------------------|
|                  |                    |                               |
|                  |                    |                               |
|                  |                    |                               |

### Read your balanced responses out loud once per day for one week.
